what_it_does: A preview card (@dsCard group="Brand") that renders the Shape system: a grid of typed geometric vessels (regular polygons 3–8 sides plus the circle), each carrying an icon, built at runtime by iterating CV_SHAPES.shapeTypes and calling CV_SHAPES.markSVG(shape) for each entry, with name, sub-line (shape · sides · icon), and meaning.
observations: Loads three JS modules: cv-icons.js, cv-vi-glyph.js, cv-shapes.js, plus colors_and_type.css and _card.css.
Grid is populated by an IIFE that maps S.shapeTypes into .type cells using template strings.
Sub-line handles the circle specially (sides shown as ∞) and singular/plural 'side(s)'.
Lead text declares the token cascade: markDefaults (tokens) ◀ shape-type ◀ markSVG(opts).
Footer cites the source registries: CV_SHAPES.geom, CV_ICONS, markDefaults → tokens, single renderer CV_SHAPES.markSVG.
All shape data (geometry, sides, icon, meaning) comes from CV_SHAPES, not hardcoded in this file.
standouts: Shapes carry typed meaning — the vessel itself signals the kind of thing inside, not decoration.
Single-renderer claim: one markSVG call can retint/restroke/reshape any vessel via the token cascade.
conventions: Data-driven rendering from a single registry (CV_SHAPES) rather than static markup — matches the project's register-as-truth law.
Tokens via var(--…); no raw color/size literals in the styles beyond layout.
notes: The brand card that demonstrates the typed-shape vessel system, rendered entirely from CV_SHAPES so the catalogue IS the display.
questions: What is the full set of shapeTypes and their meanings (defined in cv-shapes.js, not visible here)?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — subtitle (typed geometric vessels, icon inside, every treatment a token parameter) is exactly what the script renders from CV_SHAPES.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth (one renderer, one geometry registry)
Interface as projection of a registry
intention_signals: Establishes that shapes are a typed semantic vocabulary driven from one registry and one renderer, so any vessel in the product is generated, retintable, and meaning-bearing without bespoke markup.
novelty: Constraining the vessel vocabulary to regular polygons 3–8 sides (plus circle as ∞) and assigning each shape a fixed meaning, all rendered by one parametric SVG function.
contribution: Contributes the typed-shape vessel vocabulary and its single-renderer/token-cascade model that a fused system would keep as its geometric entity-marking language.
summary_for_embedding: A ConceptV brand preview card that renders the typed shape system — regular polygons 3–8 sides plus circle, each a meaning with an icon — generated from the CV_SHAPES registry via one markSVG renderer and token defaults.
intention_for_embedding: To define and demonstrate a single-sourced, token-parameterized vocabulary of meaning-bearing geometric vessels.
